items.headline: Major commercial project approved on West San Carlos Street
items.summary: A $696,570 commercial/industrial development is underway at 1300 W San Carlos St, one of San José's key commercial corridors. This represents significant new business activity in the area.

items.headline: Cupertino launches business license amnesty program for shopping centers
items.summary: The city is offering a business license amnesty program targeted at shopping center businesses, likely to help informal or non-compliant operators become legitimate. This could affect local retail compliance and tax revenue while potentially bringing underground businesses into the formal economy.

items.headline: City announces summer celebration campaign and retail incentive programs
items.summary: Cupertino is launching a 'Summer of Celebrations' shop local campaign and new economic development incentives for retail, small businesses, and startups. The program aims to boost local commerce and may include new partnerships between the city and private businesses.

items.headline: Great American Litter Pick-up approved as annual city event
items.summary: San José designated the Great American Litter Pick-up as an official annual city-sponsored special event. The city will fund the event and accept donations of materials and services to support cleanup efforts.

items.headline: City seeks input on community development grant funding
items.summary: The Housing and Human Services Commission reviewed applicants for HOME and Community Development Block Grant (CDBG) funding on March 25. These federal grants support affordable housing and community development projects in Sunnyvale.

items.headline: Planning Commission to study San Antonio Road area transportation and land use
items.summary: The Planning Commission will examine transportation alternatives and land use changes for the San Antonio Road area. This study session could reshape how the corridor develops and how residents and traffic move through that part of Palo Alto.

items.headline: New retail vitality ordinance proposed to revive struggling commercial areas
items.summary: The Commission is considering amendments to zoning and building codes to implement retail vitality policies from the Comprehensive Plan. The changes aim to support and revitalize commercial districts, potentially affecting what types of businesses can open and where.

items.headline: New electrical transmission lines planned across wastewater facility
items.summary: LS Power Grid California will install electrical transmission infrastructure across the San José-Santa Clara Regional Wastewater Facility. This infrastructure project could affect regional power distribution and utility services.
